messages.content: We refunded a customer's transaction through Razorpay's refund flow. Are Razorpay PA fees still payable on that transaction?

Yes, under Part B, Part I, Clause 3.4, Razorpay PA fees remain applicable and payable on each transaction even if you refund the customer. The clause applies whether the refund is made through normal refund channels or through Razorpay PA Group Entities' instant refund service, if used.

known_facts.fact: The merchant refunded a customer's transaction through Razorpay's refund flow.
The merchant wants to know whether Razorpay PA fees remain payable on the refunded transaction.

messages.content: A card payment on our Razorpay PA integration was authorized late, and our system never captured it. Will Razorpay automatically refund the customer, and how soon could that happen?

Yes—Under Part B, Part I, Clause 3.5, for payments that are late authorized but not captured by you, Razorpay PA may initiate an auto-refund to the customer within five days. The clause says Razorpay PA may initiate the auto-refund, so it is framed as something Razorpay PA may do rather than as a merchant-initiated refund.

messages.content: A Facility Provider has told Razorpay PA that one of our customers reported an unauthorized debit on their payment instrument. Can Razorpay PA suspend our settlements while this is being investigated?

Yes—under Part B, Part I, Clause 4.1, if a Facility Provider intimates Razorpay PA that a customer has reported an unauthorised debit of the customer's Payment Instrument, Razorpay PA is entitled to suspend settlements to you during the pendency of inquiries, investigations, and resolution by the Facility Providers. This is stated as being subject to clauses 2.1 and 2.2 of Part B, Part I, and in addition to Razorpay PA's rights under clause 16 of Part A.

known_facts.fact: A Facility Provider has intimated Razorpay PA that a customer reported an unauthorised debit of the customer's Payment Instrument.
The merchant is asking whether Razorpay PA can suspend settlements while the matter is being investigated.

Yes—under Part A General Terms and Conditions, Clause 16.1, Razorpay PA has the right to immediately suspend the Services and settlement of any monies or payments to you, without liability to you, when one of the listed events occurs.

messages.content: We operate a paid adult-content membership website with sexually suggestive imagery. Can we use Razorpay to collect subscription payments for access?

No, you cannot use Razorpay for paid access to adult-content or pornography-related memberships under Part A, Clause 17.1. That clause lists adult goods and services as prohibited, including pornography, sexually suggestive materials, and website access or website memberships of pornography or illegal sites.

messages.content: As a merchant integrating Razorpay, do we consent to Razorpay PA and its facility providers collecting and processing our personal and transaction data when we use the services?

Yes—under Part A, Clause 12.1, your use of the Services is governed by the Privacy Policy, and you consent to Razorpay PA and its Facility Providers collecting, storing, processing, and transferring personal data and transaction data in accordance with the Privacy Policy and Applicable Laws.

messages.content: We accept card payments through Razorpay. If Visa or Mastercard changes its network rules and Razorpay updates the Terms because of that, are those changes binding on us immediately?

Yes—Under Part A, Clause 9.1, Card Association Rules, amendments to the Terms required because of changes to Card Payment Network Rules are deemed binding on you with immediate effect. The clause also states that Card Networks may amend their guidelines, rules, and regulations from time to time.

Yes—under Part B, Part I, Clause 1.1, Razorpay PA facilitates collection of online and offline payments for products or services sold by you, subject to the General Terms and the Specific Terms for Payment Aggregation Services. The same clause also says that if any settlement amount is less than Rupee 1, Razorpay will endeavour to make that settlement but is not obligated to do so.

messages.content: If a customer’s bank raises a chargeback on a card payment we processed through Razorpay PA, does the ToS make us responsible for that chargeback, including international chargebacks?

Yes—under Part B, Part I, Clause 2.1, liability for a Chargeback, whether domestic or international, solely rests with you. The clause also says Razorpay PA may deduct the Chargeback Amount from Transaction Amounts upon receiving a Chargeback Request, subject to availability of funds.

messages.content: For Razorpay e-mandates, what does the Terms of Use mean by “Collection Information” that we provide for a debit?

“Collection Information” means the secured-format information or data you provide specifying the amount to be collected, details identifying the customer’s Bank Account, and the collection date. Under Part B, Part II, Clause 3, it is the information used to specify what amount should be collected, from which Bank Account, and when.

messages.content: We are a merchant planning to use Razorpay PA devices for in-store payments. Do the offline payment device terms apply to our use, and if Razorpay only supplies the device without payment aggregation, is Razorpay responsible for settling the funds?

Yes—the offline payment device terms apply to your use of the Services and Devices, and if Razorpay PA only provides the Devices without performing payment collection and aggregation services, it is not liable for settlement of the funds, under Part B, Part IA, Clause 1.2. The clause also limits use of the Services and Devices to your legitimate, bonafide, and legal business activities.

In cases where Razorpay PA is not performing any payment collection and aggregation services and has only provided the Devices, then Razorpay PA shall not be liable for settlement of the funds.

messages.content: We are a marketplace based outside India planning to use Razorpay to collect payments from customers in India. Do the PA-CB outward transaction terms apply to us, and do they override the general terms if there is a conflict?

Yes—under Part B, Part IB, Clause 1.1, the Part IB terms apply to a merchant based outside India, including a marketplace, using Razorpay Services to collect funds from customers within India for PA-CB Outward Transactions. Clause 1.1 also says Part IB is supplementary to Part A and Part I, but if an express Part IB provision conflicts with or sets a different operational or regulatory requirement, Part IB prevails to the extent necessary for PA-CB Outward Transactions.

LEG. BC 86/09.07.007/2001-02 dated April 8, 2002, and other notifications, circulars and guidelines issued by the RBI in this regard from time to time.

messages.content: We’ve already initiated a customer refund on the Razorpay Platform, but the transaction hasn’t settled to our bank account yet. Can Razorpay PA process the refund now, or do we need to wait?

messages.content: We're a merchant whose payouts were paused after Razorpay suspended our services. As CTO, I need to tell finance: what is the maximum number of days Razorpay can hold our settlement under the ToS?

Under Part A General Terms and Conditions, Clause 16.1, Razorpay PA has the right to immediately suspend both Services and settlement of monies or payments in the listed events. However, this clause does not define a maximum hold period, a fixed number of days, or a release timeline for suspended settlements. So the ToS supports that settlement may be suspended, but it does not resolve how long the hold can last; a specific duration should not be invented from this text.

messages.content: As CTO of a merchant using Razorpay PA for UPI, I need to configure fraud monitoring. What exact fraud amount threshold in the Razorpay Terms triggers our liability, and does the ToS say how that threshold is calculated?

The Razorpay ToS does not provide the exact fraud amount threshold or the calculation method. Under Part B, Part I, Clause 4.5, the ToS says you are liable if you breach the fraud amount thresholds provided under the NPCI guideline on “Fraud liability guidelines on UPI transactions” NPCI/2022-23/RMD/001, and that the decision of NPCI or the concerned acquiring bank is final and binding. The gap is that the ToS itself does not reproduce the threshold values, define the measurement period, or explain how the threshold is calculated. You would need to consult the referenced NPCI guideline or obtain confirmation from the relevant acquiring bank/NPCI rather than treating the ToS alone as a complete answer.

You hereby understand and agree that the decision of the NPCI or the concerned acquiring bank, as the case may be, shall be final and binding.

The merchant is asking whether the Razorpay ToS states the exact fraud amount threshold and its calculation method.

The ToS also makes NPCI's or the concerned acquiring bank's decision final and binding, leaving the operative threshold details outside the ToS text.

messages.content: A customer reported an unauthorised debit after Razorpay had already settled the payment to us. Can the Razorpay ToS tell us whether we or the customer’s bank ultimately bears the loss under the RBI fraud rules?

The Razorpay ToS does not fully answer who ultimately bears the loss in that post-settlement fraud scenario. Under Part B, Part I, Clause 4.2, if the fraudulent transaction amount has already been settled to you, disputes about that fraudulent transaction are to be resolved according to specified RBI notifications and other RBI notifications, circulars, and guidelines issued from time to time. The ToS gap is that it incorporates those external RBI materials by reference but does not reproduce their rules or state the final liability allocation itself. So the ToS establishes the governing external framework, but it does not, on its own, resolve whether you, the customer’s bank, or another party bears the loss.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part B SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Part I Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services, Clause 4.2, FRAUDULENT TRANSACTIONS
source_clauses.relevant_quote: If the amount in respect of the Fraudulent Transaction has already been settled to You pursuant to these Terms, any dispute arising in relation to the said Fraudulent Transaction, following settlement, shall be resolved in accordance with the RBI's notification DBR.No.Leg.BC.78/09.07.005/2017-18, dated July 6, 2017 read with RBI's notification DBOD. LEG. BC 86/09.07.007/2001-02 dated April 8, 2002, and other notifications, circulars and guidelines issued by the RBI in this regard from time to time.

ambiguity_reason.explanation: Clause 4.2 directs post-settlement fraudulent-transaction disputes to RBI notifications and other RBI guidance issued from time to time, but the ToS does not set out the contents of those external rules or determine the final liability allocation within the ToS itself.

messages.content: We’re updating our checkout as a merchant CTO. Based on Razorpay’s Terms of Use, can you tell me the exact current Card Network operational rules we must implement this week, and whether they require any specific API, 3DS, or receipt changes?

The ToS does not provide the exact current Card Network operational rule content or the specific implementation changes you must make. Under Part A, General Terms and Conditions, Clause 9.1, Card Association Rules, the ToS says Card Networks may amend their guidelines, rules, and regulations, and Razorpay may need to amend the Terms as a result, with such amendments binding on you immediately. However, Clause 9.1 does not reproduce the external Card Payment Network Rules, identify which rules are currently in force, or specify operational requirements such as API, 3DS, receipt, or checkout changes. So the ToS flags that external rule changes may bind you, but it does not resolve what the current external rules require.

source_clauses.display_citation: Part A GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS, Clause 9.1, CARD ASSOCIATION RULES
source_clauses.relevant_quote: We may be required to amend, modify or change these Terms pursuant to amendments to the Card Payment Network Rules and such amendments, if any, shall be deemed to be binding on You with immediate effect.

It does not state the substantive content of the current external Card Payment Network Rules, which network rule has changed, the effective operational requirements, or the specific product or technical changes required.

messages.content: A customer has reported an unauthorized debit, and Razorpay told us the Facility Provider is investigating. Since our settlements are currently suspended, do the Terms say the Facility Provider will decide the fraud investigation in our favor and release the settlements?

The Terms do not resolve that outcome. Under Part B, Part I, Clause 4.1, Razorpay PA is entitled to suspend settlements during the pendency of Facility Provider inquiries, investigations, and resolution. That clause explains Razorpay PA’s suspension right while the matter is pending, but it does not state how the Facility Provider must decide the investigation, whether the decision will be in the merchant’s favor, or that settlements must be released based on any particular investigation outcome. This is a genuine ToS gap, so the outcome should be confirmed with Razorpay PA or the relevant Facility Provider rather than inferred from the Terms.
